title: Crate setup, clap skeleton, and mode detection
purpose: Establish the norn-cli crate as a compilable workspace member with the full clap argument tree, subcommand stubs, mode detection logic, and exit code conventions. This is the foundation every subsequent brief depends on — nothing can be built until the crate compiles, the binary runs, and the REPL/print mode fork is decided.
task: Create a new crate crates/norn-cli/ with a Cargo.toml declaring norn as a dependency, a binary target named 'norn', and a clap-derive argument parser defining the full command tree (main flags + session/auth/mcp/doctor/completion subcommands). Implement mode detection: REPL when both stdin and stdout are TTYs and -p is not set; print mode otherwise. Subcommand handlers are stubs that print 'not yet implemented' and exit. The main command dispatches to either a placeholder REPL loop (read line, echo, repeat) or a placeholder print handler (echo prompt, exit). Exit codes: 0 success, 1 agent error, 2 argument error, 3 auth error.

requirements.title: Create norn-cli workspace crate
requirements.spec: The norn-cli crate SHALL exist as a workspace member with edition matching the workspace default. Cargo.toml SHALL declare norn as a dependency, plus clap (with derive feature), reedline, dirs, serde_json, tracing, tracing-subscriber, tokio (rt-multi-thread, macros).
acceptance: `cargo check -p norn-cli` succeeds with zero errors
`cargo clippy -p norn-cli -- -D warnings` produces zero warnings
Cargo.toml declares `norn = { path = "../norn" }` as a dependency
Cargo.toml declares a `[[bin]]` target with `name = "norn"`

requirements.title: Define clap argument structure with all flags and subcommands
requirements.spec: The CLI SHALL use clap derive macros to define: (1) A top-level Cli struct with all shared flags (-p/--print, -m/--model, --profile, -S/--system-prompt, --append-system-prompt, --allowed-tools, --disallowed-tools, --reasoning-effort, --max-turns, --timeout, -C/--working-dir, -c/--config repeatable, --rules, --variables repeatable, -e/--extension repeatable, -s/--output-schema, --event-schema repeatable, -f/--output-format, -o/--output, -q/--quiet, -r/--resume optional value, --fork optional value, --no-session, --session-name, --provider), positional PROMPT vararg, and a Subcommand enum. (2) Subcommand enum with Session(SessionCmd), Auth(AuthCmd), Mcp(McpCmd), Doctor, Completion(CompletionCmd). (3) Each sub-enum with its own clap derive fields matching DESIGN.md NC2/NC13/NC14/NC15/NC16/NC17.
acceptance: `norn --help` displays all top-level flags and subcommands
`norn session --help` displays list/show/resume/fork/export/remove subcommands
`norn auth --help` displays login/logout/status subcommands
`norn mcp --help` displays serve/connect subcommands
`norn completion --help` displays SHELL argument
`norn -p 'hello'` parses without error (print mode flag accepted)
`norn -m gpt-5.5 -s '{"type":"object"}' 'test prompt'` parses all flags correctly
`norn -c timeout=30s -c max_turns=5` accepts multiple -c flags

requirements.title: Implement mode detection logic
requirements.spec: The main function SHALL determine the execution mode as follows: (1) If --print is set: print mode. (2) If stdin is not a TTY (std::io::stdin().is_terminal() returns false): print mode. (3) If stdout is not a TTY: print mode. (4) Otherwise: interactive REPL mode. The mode detection SHALL be a pure function that accepts the parsed CLI args and TTY state and returns an enum Mode { Repl, Print }.
acceptance: `norn -p 'hello'` runs in print mode (outputs and exits)
`echo hello | norn` runs in print mode (piped stdin detected)
`norn 'hello' | cat` runs in print mode (piped stdout detected)
`norn` in a TTY starts interactive REPL mode (placeholder loop)
Mode detection is testable: a unit test verifies the logic with mock TTY state

requirements.title: Implement placeholder REPL and print handlers
requirements.spec: The main function SHALL dispatch to either a REPL handler or a print handler based on mode detection. The REPL handler SHALL read lines from stdin in a loop, echo them, and exit on '/exit' or EOF. The print handler SHALL read the positional PROMPT args, concatenate them, print them to stdout, and exit with code 0. Both are placeholders — subsequent briefs replace them with real implementations.
acceptance: `norn` in a TTY accepts input and echoes it back
`norn` exits on '/exit' input
`norn -p 'hello world'` prints 'hello world' to stdout and exits 0
Subcommand stubs print 'not yet implemented' to stderr and exit 1

requirements.title: Define exit code conventions
requirements.spec: The CLI SHALL use typed exit codes: 0 for success, 1 for agent errors (provider failure, tool error, schema unreachable), 2 for CLI argument errors (clap handles this automatically), 3 for authentication errors. An ExitCode enum SHALL be defined with From<ExitCode> for std::process::ExitCode.
acceptance: `norn -p 'hello'` exits with code 0
`norn --invalid-flag` exits with code 2 (clap default)
ExitCode enum has Success(0), AgentError(1), ArgumentError(2), AuthError(3) variants
A unit test verifies the numeric values

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement real agent execution — placeholder handlers only
SHALL NOT implement session persistence — no file I/O for sessions
SHALL NOT implement reedline integration — placeholder stdin reader only
SHALL NOT implement streaming display — placeholder echo only
SHALL NOT implement provider construction — no OpenAI or Claude Runner
SHALL NOT add any norn runtime calls (run_agent_step, Profile, etc.)
verification: cargo clippy -p norn-cli -- -D warnings produces zero warnings
cargo fmt -p norn-cli -- --check produces no diff
cargo test -p norn-cli passes all tests
norn --help displays the full command tree
No file exceeds 500 lines of code
No code in mod.rs beyond pub mod declarations and re-exports
